title: Iranian students studying abroad (esp. in the United States, 1975-2016) + absolute domestic school/teacher/student counts (1925-2023)
source_org: WENR (World Education News + Reviews, published by WES -- an accredited non-profit credential-evaluation organization), citing Institute of International Education (IIE) Open Doors annual census data and British Council international student mobility data; Wikipedia 'Education in Iran' for domestic schooling counts
time_coverage: 1925-2023 (domestic schooling); 1975-2016 (Iranian students in the US); 2007-2014 (global student mobility)
units: described per-row in CSV (persons, count, percent, ratio)
license_terms: WENR/WES editorial content used for factual citation of underlying IIE Open Doors statistics; Wikipedia content CC BY-SA
notes: Directly fills the user's explicit candidate direction #3 item: 'foreign-university-educated Iranian student counts (a well-documented Pahlavi-era phenomenon)'. This is a genuinely well-known but previously uncaptured Iran-specific phenomenon: Iran was literally the SINGLE LARGEST foreign-student-sending country to the United States for the 1974/75 through 1982/83 academic years (peaking at 51,310 students in 1979/80, per IIE Open Doors), a scale never repeated before or since by any single non-US-allied developing country -- and the subsequent 97% collapse to just 1,660 students by 1998/99 is itself a striking data point about the post-revolution rupture in US-Iran academic ties. Attempted to download the IIE Open Doors 'Historical Data' file directly from opendoorsdata.org for a complete continuous annual series (would have been far more granular than the point-snapshot figures used here) but the site returned HTTP 403 to both curl and the WebFetch tool; the WENR article's already-published point-in-time figures (sourced from the same underlying Open Doors data) were used instead as the next-best verifiable source. The domestic schooling counts (school/teacher/student ABSOLUTE numbers, not percentages) are a deliberately different angle from the enrollment RATIO series already in data/processed/macro_wdi.csv (SE.PRM.ENRR, SE.SEC.ENRR, SE.TER.ENRR, 1971-present) and from the literacy-rate figures already in data/raw/iran-white-revolution-corps/iranica-literacy-corps/ -- this dataset's 1925 baseline (3,300 schools, 110,000 students) predates both of those by decades and gives the earliest-available anchor point in the whole database for Iran's modern school system's starting scale.

failures.result: HTTP 403 Forbidden both times; used WENR's already-published point-in-time figures (same underlying IIE Open Doors source data) instead, which is a legitimate substitute but is a snapshot series rather than a continuous annual one -- a future pass with browser-based Cloudflare-bypass (per the technique documented in agent-fleet-cost-discipline memory) could retrieve the full continuous series
